seoTitle: How to Connect WordPress to Cloudflare (2026)
metaDescription: Route your WordPress site through Cloudflare, install the official plugin, connect an API token, and apply recommended caching and SSL settings.
intro: Connecting WordPress to Cloudflare unlocks edge caching, security, and SSL benefits, but getting the plugin and API token synced correctly matters. This guide covers onboarding your domain, installing the official WordPress plugin, and applying recommended settings.

steps.heading: Onboard your WordPress domain
steps.body: Add your domain to Cloudflare and select the free baseline plan to get started.

steps.heading: Update your nameservers
steps.body: Copy the nameservers Cloudflare assigns and paste them into your external domain registrar's settings.

steps.heading: Create a scoped API token
steps.body: In your Cloudflare profile, generate a dedicated WordPress-scoped API token.

steps.heading: Install the Cloudflare plugin
steps.body: In your WordPress admin panel, install and activate the official Cloudflare plugin from the Plugins menu.

steps.heading: Connect and apply recommended settings
steps.body: Paste your API token to link the plugin to your account, click 'Apply Recommended Settings' to enforce strict SSL, and toggle on dynamic edge caching.

faq.question: Do I need the official Cloudflare plugin, or can I just switch nameservers?
faq.answer: Switching nameservers alone routes your DNS through Cloudflare, but the plugin adds WordPress-specific integration, like automatic cache purging when you update content.

faq.question: What does 'Apply Recommended Settings' actually change?
faq.answer: It applies a set of Cloudflare-suggested performance and security defaults, including enforcing strict SSL modes for your site.

faq.question: Will connecting Cloudflare slow down my WordPress admin dashboard?
faq.answer: No, Cloudflare's caching is generally configured to bypass the WordPress admin area so you can still manage your site normally.

faq.question: Can I use Cloudflare with WordPress without an API token?
faq.answer: Basic DNS-level protection works without the plugin, but the WordPress-specific features like automatic cache purging require the API token connection.
